title: buxus pilosula subsp. cacuminis
intro: Buxus pilosulo subsp. cacuminis, also known as the Himalayan boxwood, is a species of flowering plant in the family Buxaceae. It has been used in traditional medicine, particularly in Ayurvedic practices, due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Buxus pilosula subsp. cacuminis are thought to include reducing fever, treating skin conditions, and providing relief from arthritis and other inflammatory conditions. The plant can be prepared as a herbal tea, powder, or oil, which can be used in various forms to leverage its medicinal properties.
